steps.title: Identify the coefficients
steps.content:
Compare the equation with standard quadratic form.
$$5x^2+7x-6=0$$
This gives $a=5$, $b=7$, and $c=-6$.

steps.title: Substitute into the formula
steps.content: Use the quadratic formula with those coefficient values. $$x=\frac{-7\pm\sqrt{7^2-4(5)(-6)}}{2(5)}$$ $$x=\frac{-7\pm\sqrt{49+120}}{10}$$ The answer is A.
